title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Infection
intro: Herbal teas for infection are a type of natural remedy that utilizes the medicinal properties of plants to treat and alleviate symptoms of infections. These teas can be effective in treating infections due to their antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the underlying causes of the infection. Some popular examples of herbal teas used to treat infections include peppermint tea, which helps to reduce fever and ease congestion, ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing swelling and pain, echinacea tea, which boosts the immune system to fight off infections, and goldenseal tea, which contains berberine that has antibacterial properties to combat bacterial infections. Additionally, other herbal teas such as yarrow tea, sage tea, and thyme tea can also be used to treat infections due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat infections, particularly those affecting the respiratory system, due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat infections by stimulating the immune system, reducing inflammation, and fighting off pathogens. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and glycosides, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which include antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Drinking Echinacea purpurea teas may help to alleviate symptoms of infections, such as colds and flu, and reduce the severity and duration of illness, making it a popular natural remedy for maintaining overall health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea root and flowers.
Combine the root and flowers in a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the mixture.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 3-5 days to help with infection.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Echinacea purpurea teas may be beneficial for the early treatment of acute upper respiratory infections, specifically for reducing the incidence, severity, or duration of such infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat infections, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions should be taken when using Echinacea purpurea teas, including avoiding consumption during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as well as taking it in moderation to minimize the risk of over-stimulation and gastrointestinal issues.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Echinacea purpurea tea for infection
Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day
Continue for 7-10 days or as directed by a doctor
Use 1-2 teaspoon of dried Echinacea purpurea root per cup of boiling water
Follow up with a doctor to monitor the infection's progress

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat infection-related ailments due to their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to combat bacterial and fungal infections by reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune system's response, ultimately promoting the healing of infected tissues. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and terpenoids, such as quercetin and bilobalide, contribute to the antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects of Ginkgo biloba teas. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat infections include reduced risk of antibiotic resistance, fewer side effects, and enhanced wound healing, making it a promising complementary therapy for infection-related ailments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) for sweetness.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add the honey (if using) and stir well before drinking.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be beneficial in treating infection-related ailments, but some possible side effects include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as hives or itching. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, take precautions like consuming it in moderation, avoiding it with other medications, and being cautious of potential interactions with blood thinners, as well as monitoring for signs of allergic reactions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Ginkgo biloba tea for infection.
Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 250-500 mg of Ginkgo biloba extract with each cup of tea.
Monitor your body's response and adjust dosage as needed.
Continue using Ginkgo biloba tea for 7-10 days or as directed.

preparation_amazon.title: Tai Chi Think Sharp Energizing Tea (Ginseng Ginkgo Biloba) 12 Bags

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas, also known as elderberry tea, have been traditionally used to treat various infections, including the flu and other respiratory issues, due to their antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of elderberry tea, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, help to inhibit the replication of viruses and reduce inflammation, thus aiding in the treatment of infections. By reducing the severity and duration of symptoms, elderberry tea helps to alleviate discomfort and support the recovery process, making it a popular herbal remedy for treating infections. The benefits of using elderberry tea to treat infections include its natural, non-invasive approach, high safety profile, and minimal side effects, making it an attractive alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried Sambucus nigra flowers in a clean pot.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and add the dried flowers.
Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flowers to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 3-4 times a day to help with infection symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Sambucus nigra teas for infection may be effective due to their ability to inhibit bacterial efflux systems, which could reduce bacterial virulence.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be an effective treatment for some infections, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. When using Sambucus nigra teas to treat infections, take precautions to avoid excessive consumption, which can lead to overstimulation of the immune system and exacerbate underlying health conditions, especially in people with compromised immune systems, pregnant or breastfeeding women, and those with certain allergies or sensitivities.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup Sambucus nigra tea 3 times a day.
Use 2 tablespoons dried Sambucus nigra flowers per cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow full flavor release.
Repeat for 7-10 days for best infection relief results.
Consult a doctor before using for children and pregnant women.

preparation_amazon.title: Elderberry Turmeric Ginger Tea - Made in USA

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas, derived from the leaves of the Eucalyptus globulus tree, have been traditionally used to treat respiratory infections such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and fight off the infection, thereby alleviating symptoms like coughing and congestion. The bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol, cineole, and flavonoids, contribute to the antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it an effective treatment for respiratory infections. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas has been shown to provide relief from symptoms and promote overall respiratory health, making it a popular natural remedy for treating respiratory infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Use 1 cup of boiling water to make tea. Pour boiling water over the leaves in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the leaves from the liquid with a strainer or tea infuser.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired. You can also add lemon or other herbs for taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with infections. Consult a doctor before using this remedy.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of pharmacopuncture", Eucalyptus globulus teas for infection may be an effective therapeutic agent due to its quorum-sensing-inhibitory and anti-biofilm activities against multidrug-resistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat the infection ailment, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications, which can lead to an increased risk of bleeding or other complications. To minimize these risks, it is essential to drink Eucalyptus globulus teas in moderation and avoid consuming it if you have any underlying health conditions, such as kidney or liver disease, or if you are taking blood thinners, as it may exacerbate these conditions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Eucalyptus globulus tea for infection.
Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 3 times a day.
Use 2-3 teaspoons of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.
Continue for 7-10 days or as directed by your doctor.

preparation_amazon.title: Jovvily Eucalyptus Leaves - 1lb - Dried - Cut & Sifted - Herbal Tea

preparations.herb_desc: Hydrastis canadensis teas, also known as goldenseal, have been traditionally used to treat infections and various ailments. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of goldenseal help to combat infections by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi. The bioactive constituents of goldenseal, including berberine, hydrastine, and hydrastinine, exhibit potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to its effectiveness in treating infections. The benefits of goldenseal teas in treating infections include reduced symptoms, faster recovery times, and a lower risk of complications, making it a popular herbal remedy for various infections and ailments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1/4 teaspoon of dried Hydrastis canadensis root.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils.
Add the dried Hydrastis canadensis root to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with infection.
preparations.preparation_study: According to scientific studies, Hydrastis canadensis teas have shown potential antibacterial activities against resistant microbes, however, more research is required to isolate phytoconstituents and understand their mechanism of action.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hydrastis canadensis teas can be used to treat infection ailments, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, particularly blood thinners. To use Hydrastis canadensis teas safely, it is essential to monitor your body's response and adjust the dosage accordingly, avoid taking it in excessive amounts, and consider the potential interactions with other substances like caffeine or certain medications.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Hydrastis canadensis for infection treatment.
Use 1/4 teaspoon of dried roots in 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Drink 2-3 cups of tea per day, 3 times a day for 7-10 days.
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage as advised by healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat various infections, particularly those caused by bacterial and viral pathogens. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to alleviate symptoms associated with infections, such as fever, cough, and sore throat. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea angustifolia teas, including alkylamides, caffeic acid, and isobutylamides, are responsible for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which aid in treating infections. Regular consumption of Echinacea angustifolia teas has been associated with benefits such as reduced severity and duration of infections, as well as enhanced immune system function.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinacea angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of water.
Combine the flowers and water in a pot, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the flowers.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 3-4 times a day, for best results.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Forschende Komplementarmedizin und klassische Naturheilkunde = Research in complementary and natural classical medicine", Echinacea angustifolia teas were used traditionally for the treatment of throat infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, especially when consumed in high doses or for extended periods, and may also interact with medications like blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Echinacea angustifolia teas safely, take them in moderation, avoid consuming them during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and be cautious when combining them with other medications, as this may exacerbate potential side effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup Echinacea angustifolia tea 3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon dried root per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons per cup as needed.
Use tea for 7-10 days for acute infection, up to 3 months for chronic.
Consult a healthcare professional for guidance and monitoring.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat infections, particularly those caused by bacterial and fungal pathogens. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and combat the infection, promoting healing and recovery. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, in yarrow tea work together to inhibit the growth of microorganisms and stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms. As a result, this herbal preparation can help to prevent the spread of infection, reduce the severity of symptoms, and promote a faster recovery, making it a valuable natural remedy for treating various infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 3-4 times a day, or as needed, to help manage infections.
Consult with a healthcare professional before using Achillea millefolium tea for medicinal purposes, especially if you have allergies or are taking medications.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMC complementary medicine and therapies", Achillea millefolium teas for infection show potential as a supplementary medicine against Clostridioides difficile infection (CDI) due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat infections, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, or interactions with other medications. Precautions should be taken when using Achillea millefolium teas, including using it in moderation, avoiding it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and monitoring blood clotting and bleeding due to its potential anti-coagulant properties.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional for Achillea millefolium tea's effectiveness on your infection.
Drink 1 cup of Achillea millefolium tea 2-3 times a day, as directed by your healthcare provider.
Start with a small dose of 1/4 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water, then adjust as needed.
Continue using Achillea millefolium tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable improvements in your infection.
Monitor your symptoms and consult your healthcare provider to adjust your treatment plan if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat malaria, an infection caused by the Plasmodium parasite. This herbal preparation contains alkaloids such as quinine and quinidine, which exhibit antiplasmodial properties, helping to inhibit the growth and replication of the parasite in the human body. Quinine in particular is known to bind to the parasite's hemoglobin, disrupting its ability to synthesize ATP and eventually leading to the parasite's death. The benefits of Cinchona officinalis teas in treating malaria include reduced parasite load, alleviation of symptoms such as fever and chills, and a lower risk of complications or mortality.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the dried bark in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the bark and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the bark.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help manage infection.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat malaria and other infections, but they may cause side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset due to the high content of quinine. To use Cinchona officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, avoid consuming it with other stimulants like caffeine and chocolate, and be cautious with the amount taken, especially for people with sensitive stomachs or allergies.

preparation_amazon.title: Quina Roja(chinona) & Palo de Víbora Tea – 100% Natural, Vegan, Hecho en México | 25 Tea Bags

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections (UTIs) due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. These properties enable Rosmarinus officinalis teas to help treat UTIs by reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting the elimination of the infection. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat UTIs include their ability to promote urinary health, reduce the risk of complications, and provide a natural and non-invasive alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup. Discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea. Stir well.
Drink 2-3 cups of tea per day for 7-10 days to help with infection.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of infection in developing countries, Rosmarinus officinalis teas for infection showed both antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, particularly when combined with Origanum syriacum, demonstrating a synergistic antimicrobial effect.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat the infection ailment, however, possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and stomach upset, as well as allergic reactions such as skin rashes and itching. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming excessive amounts, as this may lead to interactions with other medications and exacerbate conditions such as high blood pressure, and pregnant or breastfeeding women should be cautious of its use due to potential effects on fetal development.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat infections due to its antibacterial, antifungal, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of thyme, including thymol and carvacrol, help to combat infections by disrupting the cell membranes of microorganisms, thereby inhibiting their growth and reproduction. This herbal preparation helps to treat infections by promoting the healing process, reducing inflammation, and fighting off pathogens that can cause illness. The benefits of thymus vulgaris teas in treating infections include the ability to alleviate symptoms, prevent the spread of disease, and support the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for maintaining overall health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Thymus vulgaris leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for better taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with infection.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Thymus vulgaris teas for infection exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with upper respiratory tract infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat infections, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Precautions to take when using Thymus vulgaris teas for infection treatment include drinking them in moderation, avoiding use in pregnant or breastfeeding individuals, and being aware of potential interactions with blood thinners and other medications that may worsen bleeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat various infections, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of ginger tea help to reduce inflammation and combat the growth of pathogens, thereby alleviating symptoms of infection. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which help to soothe the digestive system and combat bacterial and viral infections. The benefits of ginger tea in treating infection ailments include reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and a boosted immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for maintaining overall health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale (fresh root can be used, but dried is recommended) and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot or kettle. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly.
Add 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale to the cooled water. You can add a sweetener or honey if desired.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. This will allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink while warm. You can drink 2-3 cups per day to help manage infections.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Infection and drug resistance, Zingiber officinale teas for infection have shown significant antibacterial activity against various bacteria, including Streptococcus mutans, Enterococcus faecalis, Staphylococcus spp., and Lactobacillus spp.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat infection ailments, but may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high ginger content, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize these side effects, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid taking Zingiber officinale teas on an empty stomach, and choose products that are low in ginger content or have been processed to reduce its potency.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat infections, particularly those caused by bacteria and fungi, due to their antimicrobial properties. The saponins and flavonoids present in these teas exhibit antiseptic and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to combat infection and promote healing. The bioactive constituents, such as glycyrrhizin and licorice root extract, work together to inhibit the growth of pathogens and reduce inflammation, making it an effective treatment for various infections. By consuming Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, individuals can benefit from reduced symptoms, accelerated recovery, and improved overall health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, and a tea infuser.
Boil the water and pour it over the dried root in the tea infuser.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the tea infuser.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional, to help with infections.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of the Medical Association of Thailand = Chotmaihet thangphaet, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for infection showed antimicrobial activity against bacteria that cause acute pharyngitis, particularly inhibiting S. pyogenes, S. pneumoniae, and S. mutans with MIC and MBC values ranging from 39-156 μg/ml and 78-312 μg/ml, respectively.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat various infections, but potential side effects may include increased blood pressure, fluid retention, and an irregular heart rhythm due to its high glycyrrhizin content. Precautions to take when using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas include limiting the consumption to short-term periods, monitoring blood pressure, and being cautious of its interaction with certain medications, such as diuretics and heart medications.

The bioactive constituents present in Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, possess strong antimicrobial properties that help to combat infections. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas has been found to provide relief from infection-related symptoms and promote overall health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water and a clean tea infuser.

Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat infection ailments, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation to treat infection ailments, such as starting with a low dose and gradually increasing as needed, and avoiding consumption if you have sensitive skin or allergies to lavender, as it can cause skin irritation and interact with certain medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat infections, particularly those caused by bacterial or fungal pathogens. The properties of this herbal preparation that help to treat infections include its antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which work together to inhibit the growth of microorganisms and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, and terpenes, contribute to its antimicrobial activity, helping to treat infections by disrupting the cell membrane of microorganisms and preventing them from multiplying. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat infections include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce the severity of symptoms, and prevent the development of antibiotic-resistant microorganisms, making it a valuable natural remedy for infection prevention and treatment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves.
Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the leaves to release their active ingredients.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate infection symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Melissa officinalis teas for infection treatment have a relatively high antioxidant capacity with a Trolox equivalent antioxidant capacity (TEAC) of 4.06+/-0.31 mM and a total phenolic content equivalent to 1370.09+/-41.38 microM of quercetin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat infection ailments, but may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals due to its high content of volatile oils. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dosage, avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, and not to use it for extended periods of time.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat these infections by reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and killing bacteria that cause the infection. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, contribute to their therapeutic effects by exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat respiratory infections include reducing the severity and duration of symptoms, promoting recovery, and providing a natural and non-invasive treatment option.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for a minute.
Add the gathered Salvia officinalis leaves to the cooled water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Salvia officinalis teas for infection may be useful due to its ability to revert resistance to gentamicin in Staphylococcus aureus, as demonstrated by its extract at a concentration of 0.21 g/L.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat infections, such as sore throats and coughs, due to its antibacterial properties; however, possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Precautions include using the tea in moderation, avoiding consumption by pregnant or breastfeeding women, and monitoring blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes, as excessive consumption may exacerbate the condition.

preparations.herb_desc: Andrographis paniculata teas are a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various infections, including bacterial and viral infections, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Andrographis paniculata, such as andrographolide and neoadrographolide, exhibit immunomodulatory and antioxidant activities, helping to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. By modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, Andrographis paniculata teas help to alleviate symptoms associated with infections, such as fever, cough, and sore throat. The benefits of using Andrographis paniculata teas to treat infections include reduced duration of illness, improved recovery rates, and fewer side effects compared to conventional antibiotics.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Andrographis paniculata leaves.
Add the dried leaves to 1 cup of boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Tropical biomedicine, Andrographis paniculata teas for infection show antibacterial activities, particularly against Methicillin Resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) and Pseudomonas aeruginosa, and are the most potent at a minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of 2 g/disc.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Andrographis paniculata teas can be used to treat infection ailments, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes and itching. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose, gradually increase as needed, and be cautious when consuming the tea on an empty stomach or with other medications that may interact with it.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, also known as wild marjoram, have been traditionally used to treat various infections, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce the severity of infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and alleviating symptoms such as congestion and inflammation. The bioactive constituents responsible for these effects include carvacrol, thymol, and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities. By consuming Origanum vulgare teas, individuals can benefit from their infection-fighting properties, reducing the risk of complications and promoting overall health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh or dried Origanum vulgare leaves.
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add 4-6 tablespoons of Origanum vulgare leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day to help with infection.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Origanum vulgare teas have a high antioxidant potential, with a water-phase Trolox equivalent antioxidant capacity (TEAC) of 5.87+/-0.2mM and a total phenolic content of 1653.61+/-11.52 microM.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat various infections, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. To use this herbal preparation safely, take precautions by monitoring your body's response and avoiding it if you have sensitive stomach or allergies, and do not consume it in large quantities or for extended periods.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections (UTIs) due to their diuretic and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat UTIs by increasing urine production, which helps to flush out bacteria and reduce the risk of infection, as well as by inhibiting the growth of certain bacteria that cause UTIs. The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum officinale teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to alleviate symptoms of UTIs such as pain and discomfort. The benefits of using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat UTIs include their natural and non-invasive nature, which can help to prevent antibiotic resistance and promote overall urinary health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the leaves and flowers in a pot. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea for taste, if needed. Stir well.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for the desired effect.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Expert review of clinical pharmacology, Taraxacum officinale teas for infection may have antimicrobial properties that can help combat resistant strains of microorganisms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat infections, but potential side effects may include allergic reactions, diarrhea, and stomach upset due to the high content of saponins and other compounds. When using Taraxacum officinale teas, individuals should be cautious and take note of their body's response, as excessive consumption may lead to kidney problems, and it is recommended to start with small doses to assess tolerance.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat infection-related ailments due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, including curcuminoids and polyphenols, help to inhibit the growth of pathogens and reduce inflammation, thereby treating infections effectively. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting tissue repair, Curcuma longa teas aid in the resolution of infections and promote overall health. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat infection-related ailments include reduced risk of antibiotic resistance, minimal side effects, and enhanced immune system function.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Turmeric root (Curcuma longa) to a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Steep the Turmeric in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Turmeric root.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Biological trace element research, Curcuma longa teas for infection may be an effective treatment due to their high concentration of antioxidants and their inhibitory action against most bacterial strains, including those causing gastrointestinal infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset, when used to treat infections, particularly when taken in high doses or for extended periods. To use Curcuma longa teas safely, it is recommended to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid mixing them with other medications or herbal preparations that may interact with Curcuma longa's active compounds, such as turmeric.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas have been traditionally used to treat infections due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms and reducing inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms such as fever and pain. The bioactive constituents of Thymus serpyllum teas, including thymol and carvacrol, have been found to possess potent antimicrobial activity, which aids in combating bacterial and fungal infections. The benefits of using Thymus serpyllum teas to treat infections include rapid recovery, reduced risk of complications, and minimal side effects, making it a promising natural remedy for this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus serpyllum leaves and flowers.
Combine the leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for best results.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus serpyllum teas can be used to treat various infections, but possible side effects may include dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using Thymus serpyllum teas include starting with small doses, avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and being cautious when combining with other medications, especially blood thinners, as this may increase the risk of bleeding.

preparation_study: According to Archives of family medicine, Echinacea purpurea teas for infection showed a 10-20% relative risk reduction, based on the results of this and 2 other studies, but a prophylactic effect could not be shown in this particular study.
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", teas for infection, such as those made from Huangqin (Scutellaria sp.) and Huanglian (Coptis sp.), showed in vitro activity against various microbial pathogens, with Huangqin inhibiting Klebsiella pneumoniae and Proteus vulgaris at 200 micrograms/ml.
